subject: Gaming
title: Atari vs Magnavox: The First Console Rivalry
description: A quick win: understand Atari vs Magnavox: The First Console Rivalry.

hook.text: Before Atari existed, Magnavox had the first console. Atari saw it, copied the idea, and got sued.

buildup.text: Ralph Baer's Magnavox Odyssey launched in 1972 with primitive graphics and overlay sheets for the TV.

discovery.text: Nolan Bushnell attended an Odyssey demo, then built Pong. The resemblance was obvious to everyone.

twist.text: Magnavox sued and won royalties. But Atari's superior marketing made Pong the household name.

climax.text: The first console war taught the industry a lesson: innovation matters less than execution.

punchline.text: The inventor sued. The copier became famous. A pattern was set.

quiz.question: What happened between Magnavox and Atari?
options: Atari copied the Odyssey concept and Magnavox sued successfully
